data.title: Microservices Architecture
data.description: YouTube uses a microservices architecture to handle various functions like video streaming, user management, and recommendation algorithms.
data.technical_spec: Decomposed services running in Docker containers managed by Kubernetes.

data.title: Monolithic Reduced Design
data.description: Legacy components exist which are built using a monolithic architecture, particularly in backend components.
data.technical_spec: Legacy Java and Python services with RESTful API interfaces.

data.title: Content Delivery Network (CDN)
data.description: Utilizes global CDNs for efficient video streaming and data replication.
data.technical_spec: Leverages Google Cloud CDN and other third-party CDNs for edge server locations.

data.title: Load Balancing
data.description: Load balancing is applied globally across services to ensure uptime and balanced resource utilization.
data.technical_spec: Utilizes Google Cloud Load Balancer with BGP (Border Gateway Protocol) for traffic management.

data.title: Data Storage Systems
data.description: Utilizes distributed databases for handling data relations and storing large volumes of video data.
data.technical_spec: Uses Bigtable for data storage and Spanner for transactional consistency.
